official_entry_points.note: Primary official station timetable search root for JR East.
Tokyo Station timetable list exposes Shinkansen route-family entry points including Tohoku/Hokkaido, Akita, Yamagata, Joetsu, and Hokuriku.

notes: JR East already matches the v1 ingestion model well.
Use station timetable pages to discover minute links and train-detail pages.
Likely best starting operator for v2 scale-up because train-instance extraction pattern is already proven.

official_entry_points.note: Operator context page; useful for service scale and official terminology.

notes: Official English timetable explicitly says it is a basic timetable and not all trains are listed.
This is an official but incomplete source for the all-trains v2 target.
Need a deeper official path or an additional operator-specific official source to reach full train-instance coverage.

official_entry_points.note: Official JR West Shinkansen portal with route context, station timetable search, and electronic timetable pamphlets.
Representative official station timetable page for Shin-Osaka on Tokaido/Sanyo/Kyushu Shinkansen.
Official electronic timetable pamphlet for Tokaido/Sanyo/Kyushu Shinkansen after the 2026-03-14 revision.

notes: JR West clearly exposes official station timetable pages.
Need to verify whether the station pages expose train-detail pages rich enough to reconstruct train instances directly.
Electronic timetable pamphlets may become the fallback official source if train-detail pages are insufficient.

official_entry_points.note: Official JR Kyushu train portal with timetable links for major trains.
Official Nishi Kyushu Shinkansen KAMOME page with station list, timetable, and train-number context.
Official Hakata station page with timetable entry.
Official Kagoshima-Chuo station page with timetable entry.

notes: JR Kyushu official pages expose major-train timetable pages and station timetable entry points.
Need to verify whether those official pages cover all trains or only curated major services.
Nishi Kyushu pages are especially important because v2 must preserve real names such as Kamome service numbers.

next_questions: Which official operator pages expose enough detail to reconstruct full train instances directly?
Where do we need official electronic timetable pamphlets as fallback instead of station-detail pages?
How should we unify cross-operator services while preserving real service names and numbers?
